description: Evaluates the stakeholder mapping skill across different organizational contexts, political dynamics, and change readiness scenarios. Each eval targets a specific stakeholder analysis capability.

evals.name: full-6-section-transformation
evals.prompt: Map stakeholders for a company-wide ERP migration affecting 500 employees across 4 departments.
evals.expected_output: Complete 6-section analysis: stakeholder identification with hidden stakeholder detection, influence-interest matrix, RACI, communication plan, change readiness assessment, and engagement monitoring.
expectations: Stakeholder categories cover sponsors, decision-makers, implementers, end users, and affected parties
Hidden stakeholder detection techniques applied (snowball method, meeting archaeology, veto scan)
Influence-interest matrix has 4 quadrants with engagement strategy per quadrant
RACI matrix has exactly one Accountable per deliverable/decision
Communication plan specifies channel, frequency, format, and owner per stakeholder group
Change readiness maps adoption curve and identifies resistance archetypes
Champions identified with activation plan

evals.name: highly-political-organization
evals.prompt: Build an influence matrix for a merger integration where two leadership teams are competing for control of the combined IT organization.
evals.expected_output: Handles highly political edge case: informal network analysis supplements org chart, confidential 1:1 interviews recommended, power dynamics mapping plots formal vs informal authority.
expectations: Detects highly political scenario and supplements formal org chart with informal network analysis
Power dynamics mapping plots formal authority against informal influence
Coalition building identifies natural alliances and fault lines across both organizations
Attitude overlay marks stakeholders as +, -, or ? to shape engagement tone
Influence matrix treated as confidential document
Recommends 1:1 confidential interviews for accurate power assessment

evals.name: resistance-archetype-detection
evals.prompt: Assess change readiness for adopting DevOps practices in a team with a senior architect who publicly opposes the initiative.
evals.expected_output: Change readiness assessment identifies resistance archetypes: the senior architect mapped as Blocker or Saboteur with tailored engagement strategy.
expectations: Senior architect classified under appropriate resistance archetype (Blocker or Saboteur)
Tailored response strategy matches the archetype (needs to be heard, given a role, or confronted)
Adoption curve maps the team across innovators through laggards
Champion candidates identified from early adopters
Training needs analysis addresses DevOps skill gaps
Escalation protocol defined if resistance persists after engagement

evals.name: raci-conflict-resolution
evals.prompt: Assign RACI for a cross-departmental data platform project where Engineering, Data Science, and Product all claim ownership.
evals.expected_output: RACI with clear single accountability per deliverable, conflict resolution process for overlapping claims, and escalation protocol.
expectations: Exactly one A (Accountable) assigned per deliverable despite competing claims
Decision authority framework clarifies what can be decided locally vs needs escalation
Conflict resolution process defined for disagreements between departments
Escalation protocol includes trigger criteria, path, and time limits
RACI validation step recommends review with all three department heads
Single accountability rule enforced and explained

evals.name: remote-distributed-teams
evals.prompt: Create a communication plan for a globally distributed team across US, Europe, and Asia implementing a new CI/CD pipeline.
evals.expected_output: Communication plan accounts for async channels, time zones, and cultural sensitivity in engagement approach.
expectations: Communication matrix includes async-first channels (Slack, email, recorded updates)
Time zone coverage ensures no team is systematically excluded from live meetings
Cultural sensitivity addressed: direct vs indirect feedback norms across regions
Cadence calendar aligned with phase gates and accounts for regional holidays
Feedback loops support async participation (not just live meetings)
Crisis communication plan includes rapid notification across time zones
